message:
Implement connector framework and shared abstractions

Add foundational trait and type system for agent-specific connectors,
establishing the contract that all six connector implementations
(Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cline, Amp, OpenCode) will fulfill.

Connector trait definition:
Define Connector trait with two core responsibilities per PLAN § 7.1:
- detect() -> DetectionResult: Probe filesystem and PATH to determine
  if agent is installed on the system, return detection status with
  evidence strings (e.g., "binary found at /usr/local/bin/codex",
  "data directory ~/.codex exists")
- scan(ScanContext) -> Result<Vec<NormalizedConversation>>: Parse
  agent-specific log files/databases from data_root directory, filter
  by optional since_ts timestamp for incremental updates, return
  normalized conversation objects ready for SQLite insertion

DetectionResult struct:
Simple evidence accumulator with detected boolean and evidence Vec<String>
for UI display in agent selection screen (per PLAN § 10.3 detection UI).
Provides not_found() constructor for negative detection result. Implements
Serialize/Deserialize for potential config caching (remember detected
paths across runs to avoid repeated filesystem probing).

ScanContext struct:
Shared parameters for scan operations: data_root PathBuf (agent-specific
history location like ~/.codex, ~/.gemini/tmp, VS Code globalStorage
path) and optional since_ts i64 unix millis timestamp for incremental
scanning (only parse files modified after this timestamp, critical for
performance with large corpora per PLAN § 8.2 incremental updates).

NormalizedConversation/Message/Snippet structs:
Intermediate representation for connector output, parallel to model::types
but simplified for serialization during connector->storage handoff:
- agent_slug: String identifying source tool ("codex", "cline", etc.)
- external_id: tool's session/task identifier for UNIQUE constraint
  deduplication in SQLite (per PLAN § 7.1 idempotency via source_path)
- workspace: optional project root path for filtering/grouping in UI
- source_path: absolute path to original log file or database, enables
  "jump to source" feature (open in $EDITOR at specific line)
- started_at/ended_at: conversation lifecycle timestamps for time-range
  filtering and chronological display
- metadata: serde_json::Value blob for tool-specific fields without
  polluting normalized schema
- messages: ordered vec of turns with role (user/agent/tool), author,
  timestamps, content text, and embedded code snippets

Role representation:
NormalizedMessage.role stored as String (not MessageRole enum from
model::types) to defer parsing during connector scan phase—connectors
emit raw role strings from source formats, storage layer maps to enum
during insertion for type safety in application logic. This separation
prevents connector parse failures from malformed role values (graceful
degradation: unknown role preserved as MessageRole::Other(string)).

Snippet structure:
Optional code fragment metadata attached to messages: file_path for
context (e.g., "src/main.rs edited in this turn"), start_line/end_line
for precise location, language hint for syntax highlighting in UI,
snippet_text for inline code blocks extracted from markdown-formatted
agent responses. All fields optional—not all messages contain code,
not all agents provide structured snippet metadata.

stub_connector! macro implementation:
Declarative macro generating stub implementations for all six connectors,
enabling compiles and type checking before actual parsing logic is written.
Each generated struct (CodexConnector, ClineConnector, etc.) implements:
- Default and new() for construction
- Connector trait with detect() returning not_found() and scan() returning
  empty Vec (no-op until real implementation added)

Macro instantiation for all agents:
Invoke stub_connector! six times to generate CodexConnector,
ClineConnector, GeminiConnector, ClaudeCodeConnector, OpenCodeConnector,
AmpConnector. These types are re-exported from individual module files
(codex.rs, cline.rs, etc.) in subsequent commit to maintain clean module
namespace (users import connectors::codex::CodexConnector, not
connectors::CodexConnector directly).

Rationale for intermediate types vs direct model::types usage:
Connectors output NormalizedConversation (serializable, flat structure)
rather than model::types::Conversation (includes id field assigned by
SQLite, references to Agent/Workspace via foreign keys). This separation:
- Avoids coupling connectors to database schema (can change storage
  layer without touching connector code)
- Enables potential future alternate storage backends (e.g., export to
  JSON files without SQLite dependency)
- Simplifies testing: connectors return plain structs assertable with
  serde_json snapshot tests (insta crate), no database mocking required

Integration with storage layer:
storage::sqlite::SqliteStorage::insert_conversation_tree (implemented
in next commit) will consume NormalizedConversation and map fields to
model::types::Conversation with database-assigned IDs. The normalized
structs are transient—live only during scan->insert pipeline, never
persisted directly.

Error handling strategy:
Connector scan() returns anyhow::Result allowing each implementation
to use .context() for rich error messages (e.g., "failed to parse
Codex rollout-abc123.jsonl: unexpected end of file at line 42"). Errors
are non-fatal at indexer orchestration layer—failed scan of one source
file logs warning and continues to next file, preventing single malformed
log from halting entire index operation (per PLAN § 8 error recovery).

This commit establishes the connector framework without implementing
real detection or parsing logic. Subsequent commits will replace stub
implementations with actual filesystem probing and format-specific
parsing (Codex JSONL, Gemini JSON, Cline task directories, OpenCode
SQLite, etc.) per PLAN § 14.4-14.6 implementation order.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (connectors
layer), § 7.1 (shared abstractions), § 7.2-7.7 (per-connector specs),
model/types.rs (parallel domain model), README.md (connectors/ overview).
